_comment: Mutation testing runs a subset of the codebase — hooks + critical components that silent coverage would miss. Keep the `mutate` globs narrow: full-repo mutation runs are ~10min+ and the signal-to-noise drops fast on pure UI.
testRunner_comment: Stryker runs `vitest --run` for each mutant; the vitest-runner plugin handles that.
vitest_comment: A Stryker-only vitest config that drops babel-plugin-react-compiler. The compiler and Stryker's instrumenter both rewrite the AST and produced duplicated `stryNS_9fa48` declarations that broke parsing.
mutate_comment: Scoped to .ts files. Mutating .tsx files triggers a Stryker-instrumenter + @stryker/instrumenter bug (duplicate `stryNS_9fa48` declarations in JSX-transformed output). The hooks give us the highest-value coverage — business logic that silent tests can miss.
thresholds_comment: Break at 40 — the hook tests currently only cover the delete mutations we added; raising the bar as we write tests for the other hooks (create, send, etc.). Target: 60 low / 80 high once chat+knowledge hook suites fill in.
ignorePatterns_comment: Keep test files IN the sandbox — Stryker needs them to run assertions against mutants. The .test.tsx exclusions are pre-existing failing tests (missing NextIntlClientProvider mock context); they would prevent the dry run baseline from passing. api-client.test.ts has one pre-existing failure expecting a different error message than the implementation — excluded so Stryker's baseline clears.
